advantages: Outputs calibrated-style probabilities in (0, 1) via sigmoid — useful for ranking, threshold tuning, and ensembling.
Trained with convex BCE objective in w for fixed data — no local minima from the loss landscape itself (unlike deep nets).
Interpretable coefficients: each weight shows direction and relative strength of feature influence on log-odds.
Fast to train and score on CPU; strong tabular baseline before gradient-boosted trees or neural nets.
Linear decision boundary is easy to visualize in 2D and audit for fairness or regulatory review.
disadvantages: Only learns linear boundaries in feature space — cannot separate XOR-like patterns without feature engineering or kernels.
Sensitive to outliers and multicollinearity in features; extreme x values can push ŷ arbitrarily close to 0 or 1.
Requires sufficient labeled data; underfits complex nonlinear structure that trees or deep models capture.
Assumes log-odds are linear in raw features — poor fit when the true decision surface is highly curved.
Class imbalance can bias ŷ toward the majority class unless weighted BCE, resampling, or threshold adjustment is applied.
